trade.summary: Strong bilateral trade relationship. The UK exports financial services, whisky, pharmaceuticals, and luxury goods to Japan. Japan exports automobiles, electronics, and precision machinery to the UK. The UK-Japan Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA, 2021) secured and enhanced trade ties post-Brexit.

diplomatic.summary: UK-Japan relations have been elevated to a Global Strategic Partnership. Both countries share strong alignment on rules-based international order, Indo-Pacific security, and NATO-adjacent cooperation. Japan-UK ties strengthened significantly through the GCAP fighter programme, the Hiroshima Accord (2023), and Japan's new global security role. The UK is Japan's primary European ally.

military.summary: Among the deepest UK military partnerships outside traditional allies. GCAP (Global Combat Air Programme) is the flagship — UK, Japan, and Italy jointly developing next-generation fighter jet. Reciprocal Access Agreement (2023) allows each country's forces to deploy from the other's bases. Joint exercises increasing in scale and frequency. Both countries deeply aligned on China and Russia threat assessments.

financial.summary: London is the primary European financial centre for Japanese banks and insurers. Japanese companies (Toyota, Honda, Nissan) have major UK manufacturing operations. Japanese institutional investors hold significant UK gilts and equities. UK financial services deeply integrated with Japanese capital markets.

energy.summary: Some bilateral LNG trade (UK imports, Japan exports via trading). Cooperation in offshore wind technology and hydrogen. UK nuclear expertise (Rolls-Royce SMRs) is of interest to Japan as it restarts nuclear capacity.

scientific.summary: Strong research partnership. GCAP involves deep technology exchange. Cooperation in AI, quantum computing, life sciences, and materials. UK-Japan Science and Innovation Council active. Many Japanese researchers work in UK universities. Joint projects in fusion energy (ITER-related).
